pi_items.source: Task specification - verified in task.json, setup_task.sh, verifier.py, and export_result.sh

All three airports verified via FAA/AirNav: KPAO is Palo Alto Airport (37.461111, -122.115056), KLVK is Livermore Municipal Airport (37.69340, -121.82040), KWVI is Watsonville Municipal Airport (36.93570, -121.78960)
Great circle distance calculation using verified airport coordinates: KPAO→KLVK (19.8 nm) + KLVK→KWVI (45.5 nm) + KWVI→KPAO (35.2 nm) = 100.5 nm total
Great circle distance calculation using verified airport coordinates: KPAO→KLVK (19.8 nm) + KLVK→KWVI (45.5 nm) + KWVI→KPAO (35.2 nm) = 100.5 nm total

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a flight plan in Avare (free open-source aviation GPS app) with four waypoints forming a round-trip: KPAO (Palo Alto Airport), KLVK (Livermore Municipal Airport), KWVI (Watsonville Municipal Airport), and back to KPAO. All airport codes are real FAA-registered airports in California. The plan name must be exactly 'BayAreaTraining'. The metadata claims the total distance should be between 130-160 nautical miles, but the actual great-circle distance for this round-trip route is 100.5 nautical miles (KPAO→KLVK: 19.8 nm, KLVK→KWVI: 45.5 nm, KWVI→KPAO: 35.2 nm), which falls significantly short of the claimed minimum.
